text: Course: CME222 Mass Transfer 2026/04 Peng Chee Tan

list_items: • Apply the fundamental knowledge of mass transfer in practical problem
• Distinguish the mechanisms of mass transfer and calculate the mass transfer diffusion coefficients of gases, liquids, and solids
• Analyse diffusional process and calculate the flux in one phase and two-phase system

• Basic concept of mass transfer
• Momentum, energy and mass transfer analogies

• James Welty, Gregory L. Rorrer, David G. Foster (2019). Fundamentals of Momentum, Heat and Mass Transfer. 7th Edition, Wiley
• Theodore L. Bergman, Adrienne S. Lavine, Frank P. Incropera, David P. DeWitt (2016). Fundamentals of Heat and Mass Transfer 8th Edition. Wiley.
• R. Byron Bird, Warren E. Stewart, Edwin N. Lightfoot, Daniel J. Klingenberg (2014). Introductory Transport Phenomena, 1st Edition, Wiley.
• Basic Concept of Mass Transfer
• Momentum, Energy, and Mass Transfer Analogies

text: These three transports should be studied together:

list_items: • Frequently occur simultaneously in industrial process
• The basic equations are closely related
• Mass transfer is defined as the transports of one or more chemical species in fluid mixture due to some driving force
• Driving force: concentration differences, temperature differences, pressure differences, difference due to gravity, magnetic or electrical field

text: There is a natural tendency for mass to transfer in a system with two or more components when their concentrations vary from point to point, minimizing these differences

list_items: • Separation of uranium isotopes
• Liquid uranium hexafluoride feed into the space between two concentric vertical pipes
• Upward flow on hot side, downward flow on cold side
• Heavier U-238 accumulate near cold side
• Lighter accumulate near hot side
• Taller column → more efficiency separation
• Charged particle send through magnetic field
• Charged particle experiences a force when moving through a magnetic field
• Ion deflected
• Ions, atoms or molecules that have a charge, can be affected by an electrical driving force
• The direction of the electrical driving force results in the ion moving toward a region where the opposite charge exists
• Cl- is oxidized to chlorine at anode (A)
• Selective membrane allows Na+ to freely flow across
• At cathode, water is reduced to hydroxide and hydrogen gas
• Perfume presents a pleasant fragrance that is imparted throughout the surrounding atmosphere
Drying of clothes
Evaporation of water spilled on the floor when water molecules diffuse into the air surrounding it
• Dispersion of air pollutants released from a power plant smoke stack into the environment
• Diffusion is the transport of molecules, ions, or small particles from a region of relatively high concentration into a region of lower concentration
• In the absence of a concentration gradient, molecules continue to move randomly (Brownian motion), but no net mass transfer occurs and the system remains uniform
Mass diffusion is in the direction of decreasing concentration
After a sufficient time, uniform concentrations of A and B are achieved
There is no net transport of species A or B across the imaginary plane

text: Difference between diffusion in solid, liquid & gas????
High concentration gradient > Small concentration gradient
Concentration difference of a solute between two points in space
Stirring helps the sugar molecules transfer to the bulk water much faster

list_items: • Mass transfer between a moving fluid and a surface or between immiscible moving fluids
• Affected by the dynamics characteristic of the flowing fluid
• Fluid motion combines with diffusion to promote the transfer of a species for which there exists a concentration gradient
• Analogous to convection heat transfer
• Water evaporates faster in the presence of wind
• Sublimation of naphthalene (solid form) into the moving air
• Spreading of water pollutant in flowing river

text: Application of Mass Transfer in Industrial Process

list_items: • Separation of component from gas stream using a solvent
• Example: to capture volatile organic compound, absorption of CO2 from flue gas

text: • To recover the solvent used in absorption
Mass transfer between vapor and liquid at each stage

list_items: • Separate component based on its relative solubilities in two immiscible solvents
Net transfer of component from one liquid to another liquid
Example:
• Extraction of penicillin from fermentation broth using solvent such as butyl acetate
• Extraction of acetic acid from water using an organic solvent
Or known as solid-liquid extraction
• Extract component out from solid using solvent
Example:
Extraction of caffeine from tea/coffee bean using solvent
• Oil extraction from soybeans, cotton seeds, peanuts using hexane
• Extraction of pharmaceutical product from plant roots, leaves, and stems
Gold is leached from its ore using aqueous sodium cyanide solution
Dry the material in industry
Example rotary drum dryer, flash dryer
• Direct (contact with heating stream) or indirect type
• Water separation: Removal of dye, oil, heavy metal contaminant from effluent, produced water
• Gas separation: CO2 removal, biogas upgrading, hydrogen recovery
• Others: Concentration of product, clarification of fruit juice

text: Momentum, Energy, and Mass Transfer Analogies

table_body:
Transport PhenomenonGoverning LawDriving Potential
Mass TransferFick's Law of DiffusionConcentration Gradient, dCb/dx
Heat TransferFourier's law of conductionTemperature Gradient, dT/dx
Momentum TransferNewton's Law of ViscosityVelocity Gradient, du/dy

list_items: • All three transports can be contributed by molecular transport and bulk flow
• Mass : Diffusion vs Convection
• Heat: Conduction vs Convection
• Momentum: Viscous vs Convection
